elements.sent-2411-001: In recognition that this is my one chance to use whichever justification, spacing, writing style, text size, and/or textfont that I want to while still keeping my headings and margins consistent.
elements.sent-2411-002: Organizations for writers palliate the writer's loneliness, but I doubt if they improve his writing.
elements.sent-2411-003: He grows in public stature as he sheds his loneliness and often his work deteriorates.
elements.sent-2411-004: For he does his work alone and if he is a good enough writer he must face eternity, or the lack of it, each day.
elements.sent-2411-005: There is no indication about how it is to be typeset.
elements.sent-2411-006: Given that, one is forced to simply typeset it and hope it is accepted.

elements.sent-2411-008: I would like to acknowledge Professor Eta Theta for his support as the chair of my committee.
elements.sent-2411-009: Through multiple drafts and many long nights, his guidance has proved to be invaluable.
elements.sent-2411-010: I would also like to acknowledge the ``Smith Clan'' of lab~28, without whom my research would have no doubt taken fives times as long.
elements.sent-2411-011: It is their support that helped me in an immeasureable way.

elements.sent-2411-021: The abstract is limited to 350 words for a doctoral dissertation.
elements.sent-2411-022: It should consist of a short statement of the problem, a brief explanation of the methods and procedures employed in generating the data, and a condensed summary of the findings of the study.
elements.sent-2411-023: The abstract may continute onto a second page if necessary.
elements.sent-2411-024: The text of the abstract must be double spaced.
elements.sent-2411-025: No formatting guidelines appear so presumably, it should be formatted like an ordinary chapter.
elements.sent-2411-026: It should appear after the \verb!\mainmatter! macro because it should start on page~1.. \end{dissertationintroduction.}
elements.sent-2411-027: The purpose of this page is to illustrate an ordinary page of text in a doctoral dissertation or master's thesis.
elements.sent-2411-028: All pages of the doctoral dissertation or master's thesis must be kept within the margins of 1.5'' on the left, 1'' on the right, 1'' on the top and 1.25'' on the bottom.
elements.sent-2411-029: All text must be double spaced except as indicated below.
elements.sent-2411-030: It is recommended that to increase the margins as paper can shift in a printer and as some photocopiers tend to increase the image being copied.
elements.sent-2411-031: The first line of each paragraph must be indented at least one 0.5'' tab, as done here.
elements.sent-2411-032: This text is intended to be a part of the dissertation, for a doctoral student, or the thesis if you are receiving a master's degree, and now a quote is included here:.
elements.sent-2411-033: All quotes of more than six lines, even though this one is not, are to be indented 0.5'' from the left and 0.5'' from the right.
elements.sent-2411-034: These longer quotes are to be single spaced.
elements.sent-2411-035: Don't forget to adjust for proper spacing after the last line of the quoted material.
elements.sent-2411-036: The rest of the paragraph would continue as so.
elements.sent-2411-037: This demonstrates how OGS wants figures and tables formatted.
elements.sent-2411-038: For figures, the caption goes below the figure and ``Figure'' is in bold.. See Figure~\ref{fig:zen .}
elements.sent-2411-039: Tables are formatted with the caption above the table.. See Table~\ref{tab:bad .}
elements.sent-2411-041: It should probably be formatted like Table~\ref{tab:good instead.}
elements.sent-2411-042: For facing caption pages, see Table~\ref{tab:facing .}
elements.sent-2411-043: Of course, facing caption pages are vaguely ridiculous and my implementation of them in the class file is by far the most brittle part of the implementation.
elements.sent-2411-044: It's entirely possible that something has changed and these don't work at all.
elements.sent-2411-045: I implemented it merely for the challenge.
